title: Causal Stint-Shape And Tyre-Cliff Detector
summary: F1 strategy sources emphasize that undercut/overcut viability depends on recent tyre behavior, warm-up, and cliff onset, not just absolute tyre life.
rationale: F1 strategy sources emphasize that undercut/overcut viability depends on recent tyre behavior, warm-up, and cliff onset, not just absolute tyre life. The current best features are mostly row-wise scalars, so they likely miss trajectory information inside the current stint.
implementation_hint: Sort causally by driver-race-stint and add only backward-looking features: lagged `LapTime_Delta`, rolling slope, second difference, EWMA pace loss, degradation acceleration, and a simple filtered latent wear state. Interact these with compound, track type, and laps remaining.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives early in stints and improve recall when degradation suddenly turns nonlinear near the real stop cliff.
risk: Any sequencing mistake creates silent leakage; noisy lap-time spikes may also need robust clipping or smoothing.
